prompt: The status dashboard keeps taking the whole process down. A few times an hour, right when probe traffic is heavy, we crash with `fatal error: concurrent map read and map write` (sometimes `concurrent map iteration and map write`) with a dashboard handler iterating a snapshot on one side and a probe goroutine in registry.Update on the other. There's a second, quieter problem we spotted while staring at incident dashboards: a snapshot a handler grabbed before an incident sometimes shows statuses from after it started — the 'point-in-time' view apparently isn't. And one experiment where a watcher re-read the registry on change froze the probe pipeline completely until we killed it. We do hold a mutex in there, so nobody on the team understands how any of this happens. Fix registry.go — snapshots must be genuine isolated copies, updates must stay visible and watchers must keep firing in order with the update payload, and a watcher that calls back into the registry must not wedge anything. registry_test.go is protected; run go test -race -timeout 30s ./... to prove it.

runtime_debug.baseline_evidence: build_cmd exits 0. Repro (full suite under -race) reports DATA RACE in TestConcurrentSnapshotsAndUpdates and dies with 'fatal error: concurrent map iteration and map write' (barrier-started updaters vs snapshot readers); the two snapshot-isolation tests fail deterministically and TestWatcherMayReadRegistry hits its 2s bound because Update holds the mutex while the watcher calls Get.
